decisions: ACCEPT M2-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 and route to lead_orchestrator for integration and formal milestone closure.
Do not start M3 from this QA review. taskctl ready produced no output and project/goal-mode-state.json has next_ready_tasks empty.
Accept H-001/H-002 only as initial M2 evidence, matching docs/GATE-M2.md; the gate must not be represented as final full-scale release performance proof.
Accept that both M1 follow-ups are closed for M2 exit: Chromium/Electron host hash canaries match Node baseline, and sim-core/content-runtime boot-boundary contract testing is accepted.
Accept that changed paths comply with M2-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 allowed/forbidden scope; no product/package/content-source/roadmap/ADR/CI/prior-gate path drift was found.
risks: H-001/H-002 remain initial pressure evidence with documented limits; future full-scale/live projection claims need renewed measurements.

QA JSON validation found 59 unique thread IDs in the current thread registry while docs/GATE-M2.md records the lead supplemental validation result as 58. This is non-blocking coordination metadata drift and did not affect task status, handoff validity, route target, or M2 evidence.
Artifact-producing validation commands wrote ignored build/package outputs, but follow-up git status showed no tracked forbidden-path drift.
requested_action: Lead should integrate this ACCEPT handoff, mark the M2 exit gate accepted through the normal closure path, and keep M3 blocked until formal lead milestone closure is recorded.
